text.title: Evaluation Metrics for LLMs Core Rules

rule.statement: Metric card MUST consider all six dimensions: accuracy, quality, latency, cost, safety, reliability.
rule.rationale: Single-dimension cards miss tradeoffs (e.g. faster + cheaper but more toxic).

rule.statement: Each dimension MUST resolve to a concrete metric OR an explicit 'n/a — out of scope' note.
rule.rationale: Empty cells in a card are interpreted as 'forgot to think about it'.

rule.statement: Quality metric MUST use a 1-5 rubric with anchored examples per score, not free-form 'rate this'.
rule.rationale: Anchored rubrics give reproducible scores; free-form does not.

rule.statement: Each metric MUST carry a threshold (acceptance criterion).
rule.rationale: Without thresholds, metrics are observed but never acted on.

rule.statement: Safety + reliability MUST be hard floors (regression below floor blocks release).
rule.rationale: Quality regressions can be tolerated; safety regressions cannot.
